case_title: ** TRANSFERRED TO SAN MATEO PROBATE DIVISION ** RONALD DILLER VS. THE HELEN DILLER FAMILY FOUNDATION ET AL

docket_entries.description: NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF
SUMMONS ISSUED TO PLAINTIFF DILLER, RONALD , INDIVIDUALLY, AND AS SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST AND REPRESENTATIVE OF HELEN DILLER, DECEASED

REPLY IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO SEAL PORTIONS OF DEFENDANTS MOTION TO TRANSFER AND SUPPORTING DOCUMENTS (TRANSACTION ID # 62621308) FILED BY DEFENDANT THE HELEN DILLER FAMILY FOUNDATION THE HELEN DILLER FOUNDATION JACLYN SAFIER, INDIVIDUALLY, AS TRUSTEE OF THE 1981 DNS TRUST AND AS EXECUTRIX OF THE ESTATE OF SANFORD DILLER

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT THE HELEN DILLER FAMILY FOUNDATION, THE HELEN DILLER FOUNDATION, JACLYN SAFIER'S, MOTION TO TRANSFER CASE TO PROBATE DEPARTMENT OF SAN MATEO SUPERIOR COURT IS CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 14, 2018 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPT. 504 BEFORE THE HON. JUDGE BOLANOS. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO GIVE NOTICE TO OPPOSING SIDE. PARTIES TO BRING THEIR OWN COURT REPORTER. ON THE 2 MOTIONS TO SEAL, THE COURT WILL SIGN THE VERSION OF THE PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED BY PLAINTIFF IF THERE IS NO OBJECTION FROM DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL. IF THERE IS AN OBJECTION FROM THE OPPOSING SIDE, THE COURT WILL SIGN THE PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS THE COURT'S RULING VERBATIM. JUDGE: SUZANNE R. BOLANOS, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/SRB)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT THE HELEN DILLER FAMILY FOUNDATION, THE HELEN DILLER FOUNDATION, JACLYN SAFIER'S, ADMINISTRATIVE MOTION TO SEAL PORTIONS OF REPLY MEMORANDUM IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO TRANSFER CASE TO PROBATE DEPARTMENT OF SAN MATEO SUPERIOR COURT AND SUPPORTING DOCUMENTS.THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT JACLYN SAFIER'S MOTION TO SEAL PORTIONS OF DEFENDANT'S REPLY MEMORANDUM IS DENIED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS) THE COURT WILL SIGN THE VERSION OF THE PROPOSED ORDERS SUBMITTED BY PLAINTIFF IF THERE IS NO OBJECTION FROM DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL. IF THERE IS AN OBJECTION, THE COURT WILL SIGN THE PROPOSED ORDERS WHICH REPEAT THE COURT'S RULING VERBATIM. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: SUZANNE R. BOLANOS, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/SRB)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-14-2018 Off Calendar

Defendant The Helen Diller Family Foundation, The Helen Diller Foundation, Jaclyn Safier'S, Motion To Transfer Case To Probate Department Of San Mateo Superior Court
Off Calendar Nov-14-2018 Granted Per Written Order

Administrative Motion To Seal Portions Of Reply Memorandum In Support Of Motion To Transfer Case To Probate Department Of San Mateo Superior Court And Supporting Documents
Off Calendar Nov-08-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Eric Rowen, Esq. and Breanna Brewer, Esq. 310-586-3855 of GreenbergTraurig for Plaintiff.
No other Appearances.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. The Court reminds Counsel re: procedures for contesting tentative rulings. Plaintiff contests the ruling on the motion to transfer and requests a different version of the proposed orders to seal to be signed.

DEFENDANT THE HELEN DILLER FAMILY FOUNDATION, THE HELEN DILLER FOUNDATION, JACLYN SAFIER's, Administrative Motion To Seal Portions Of Reply Memorandum In Support Of Motion To Transfer Case To Probate Department Of San Mateo Superior Court And Supporting Documents.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Jaclyn Safier's motion to seal portions of Defendant's reply memorandum is DENIED. Defendants have not satisfied their burden of showing substantial probability of prejudice if the sealing is not ordered under NBC Subsidiary (KNBC-TV), Inc. v. Superior Court (1999) 20 Cal.4th 1178. General allegations of settlors' intent and potential interference with charitable operations is not sufficient to overcome the public right to access this information. (See Universal City Studios, Inc. v. Superior Court (2003) 110 Cal.App.4th 1273, 1283-84 (denying sealing despite contractual obligation to avoid disclosing settlement agreement because the defendant did not show substantial prejudice to its business interests).) Moreover, the parts of the trust agreements sought to be sealed are not narrowly tailored and the redactions in the motions contain nonsensitive information.

The Court will sign the version of the proposed orders submitted by Plaintiff if there is no objection from Defendant's Counsel. If there is an objection, the Court will sign the proposed orders which repeat the Court's ruling verbatim.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/SRB)

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Jaclyn Safier's motion to seal portions of Defendant's motion is DENIED. Defendants have not satisfied their burden of showing substantial probability of prejudice if the sealing is not ordered under NBC Subsidiary (KNBC-TV), Inc. v. Superior Court (1999) 20 Cal.4th 1178. General allegations of settlors' intent and potential interference with charitable operations is not sufficient to overcome the public right to access this information. (See Universal City Studios, Inc. v. Superior Court (2003) 110 Cal.App.4th 1273, 1283-84 (denying sealing despite contractual obligation to avoid disclosing settlement agreement because the defendant did not show substantial prejudice to its business interests).) Moreover, the parts of the trust agreements sought to be sealed are not narrowly tailored and the redactions in the motions contain nonsensitive information.

The Court will sign the version of the proposed orders submitted by Plaintiff if there is no objection from Defendant's Counsel. If there is an objection, the Court will sign the proposed orders which repeat the Court's ruling verbatim.
